era: Hip-Hop. E=6 -- independent #1 in the digital era. HALFWAY MARK (180/360 = 50.0%). Hip-hop era closes (99-180, 81 degrees). Beacon Hill enters. v1.49.320.

location.origin: Beacon Hill, Seattle (new neighborhood) / Capitol Hill
location.formative: Seattle hip-hop scene. Capitol Hill. Beacon Hill. Community activism.

members.name: Macklemore
members.note: Beacon Hill. Independent. 'Thrift Shop' + 'Can't Hold Us' #1. 4 Grammys. HALFWAY MARK.

associated_musicians.name: Ryan Lewis
associated_musicians.relationship: Producer and collaborator. Degree 160. Produced 'Thrift Shop,' 'Can't Hold Us,' 'Same Love,' and *The Heist*. The full creative engine.

associated_musicians.name: Sir Mix-a-Lot
associated_musicians.relationship: Predecessor. Degree 179. Two eras of Seattle independent hip-hop at #1. Lineage from 179 to 180.

theory_nodes.concept: HALFWAY MARK -- degree 180 of 360 = 50.0%. The exact midpoint of the engine. The first half of the circle is complete. 180 artists, 180 species, 180 degree pairs documented.
examples: Hip-hop era (99-180) spans exactly to the midpoint.
Threshold between first half and second half.
theory_nodes.description: HALFWAY MARK. The engine's midpoint. 50.0% complete.

theory_nodes.concept: Independent #1 in the digital era -- back-to-back #1 hits with no major label. Digital distribution. Macklemore LLC. 1.8B+ YouTube views. The model proved that independent artists could compete at the highest commercial level.
theory_nodes.description: Independent #1 in digital era. Two hits. No major label.

genealogy.relationship: Macklemore at 180. Digital-era independence at commercial peak.

theory_nodes.concept: Hip-hop era closes (99-180) -- 81 degrees. The longest single-genre era in the engine. From underground pioneers to national superstars. The era reaches its natural endpoint at the halfway mark.
examples: Degrees 169-180: post-era gradient extending to midpoint.
theory_nodes.description: Hip-hop era closes. 81 degrees. Longest genre era.

genealogy.relationship: Macklemore at 180. Era endpoint coincides with midpoint.

theory_nodes.concept: Beacon Hill enters -- new Seattle neighborhood in the engine. One of Seattle's most diverse communities. Geographic expansion: Central District, Capitol Hill, Ballard, University District, Tacoma, Beacon Hill.
examples: Distinct from Central District, Capitol Hill, Ballard.
theory_nodes.description: Beacon Hill enters. New neighborhood. Geographic expansion.

genealogy.relationship: Macklemore at 180. Beacon Hill enters at the midpoint.

cross_references.relationship: LINEAGE! Two eras of Seattle independent hip-hop at #1. Mix-a-Lot (E=7, architect) to Macklemore (E=6, apex performer). 179→180 = pioneer to modern.
PARTNERSHIP! Lewis produced The Heist. The Macklemore & Ryan Lewis collaboration = the full creative engine behind the #1 hits.
NASTYMIX LINEAGE! Kid Sensation (177) → Sir Mix-a-Lot (179) → Macklemore (180). Three degrees spanning Seattle hip-hop's full history.
CONSCIOUS HIP-HOP! Both socially engaged. Blue Scholars (156) and Macklemore (180). Different scales, same commitment to meaning.
FULL ARC! From degree 1 to degree 180. The first half of the circle. 180 artists. The engine's midpoint reached.
